claim: Intercept right at the booth, right after the demo, respondent is rushing to a meeting in 7 minutes
quote: I'm really in a rush, I've got a meeting in another hall in seven minutes

claim: Micro-episode: just tried the demo CRM on the tablet at the booth
quote: You just tried the demo version of our CRM on the tablet at the booth

claim: Immediate reaction — positive surprise at the loading speed
quote: I was pleasantly surprised it didn't lag

claim: Immediate reaction reinforced by a second in-the-moment mention
quote: Right in the moment I thought "oh, snappy"

claim: Low readiness for conversation — respondent is visibly rushed from the very first lines

quote: Go ahead, but I really am running now

claim: Rush is reconfirmed again near the end
Brand was not known beforehand, heard the name only just now at the booth
quote: Nothing at all, zero, I heard the name itself for the first time literally a minute ago

claim: Immediate follow-up intent — will deliberately not come back, but doesn't rule out an incidental drop-by
quote: Probably not, our schedule's packed, but if there's time at lunch — I'll swing by

claim: POSITIVE valence based on the first immediate reaction (I3), not on the later rationalization
quote: I was pleasantly surprised it didn't lag

claim: Flip marker: respondent later discounts that same reaction himself — gap between in-the-moment reaction vs. rationalization
quote: speed is nice of course, but it doesn't decide anything by itself

claim: Respondent himself explains the gap: in the first second he was glad simply because it didn't freeze
quote: I guess in that first second I was just happy because it didn't freeze, but once I thought about it — there's plenty of systems like that on the market

claim: LOW reliability of the answer — respondent explicitly asks not to go deeper because he's rushing
quote: that's not important right now, let's move on, I really am running

claim: DISCOVERED BY ACCIDENT — ended up at the booth not on his own initiative, a colleague brought him
quote: My colleague dragged me over to the booth, I wouldn't have come on my own

claim: The effect trigger was specifically the loading speed against the backdrop of expected long loads at other booths
quote: I usually wait like ten seconds for stuff to load at trade shows

claim: Respondent directly admits he didn't have time to assess the interface itself — the effect isn't about the content
quote: Well I didn't really get a proper look

claim: Main insight: a positive but provisional reaction given insufficient time to evaluate
quote: Positive, but that doesn't mean we'll buy it, I'll be honest, I saw way too little
